name: Hayah Console
description: Dense product-UI theme for the surfaces people stare at all day — CRMs, dashboards, admin panels, client portals, internal tools. The only variant without a display font: restraint is the identity. Quiet cream-and-white chrome, a full semantic status system, table and data-viz tokens the marketing variants don't carry, and the brand mark working as functional vocabulary (dot = status, arc = progress).
mood: Precise, calm, legible — mission control in daylight
role: The instrument panel — where the work happens. Quiet on purpose.
inspiration: Linear's app (not its landing page), Stripe Dashboard, Height — interfaces that disappear into the task

signature.dotStatus: The logo's coral dot is the status language: a 6px filled circle precedes every stateful entity (deal, run, booking, sync). Colors map to the semantic set; live/in-progress states pulse (opacity 1 → 0.5 → 1, 2s loop, respects prefers-reduced-motion). The brand mark stops being decoration and becomes vocabulary.
signature.arcProgress: Circular progress and the loading spinner are the logo arc: a quarter-arc stroke that sweeps to full circle as progress completes, coral dot at the leading end. Replaces every generic spinner. Linear progress bars stay plain aqua per system animation rules.
signature.noDisplay: No display font, deliberately. Page titles are Geist 600 at 20px. The single exception: metric values use Space Grotesk (the system's shared data voice, owned by Bento). If a Console screen starts wanting a hero, it is a landing page — use Bento or Classic.

status.notes: Danger is red-shifted coral, warning is amber between coral and cream — both derived from the palette, neither collides with the coral CTA. Coral dot is reserved for live states (it is the logo dot).

dataViz.notes: Categorical order is fixed — series 1 is always aqua, series 2 always coral. Charts on white cards only, never on pearl.

typography.notes: tabular-nums (font-variant-numeric) on every cell, metric, and timestamp — data must align.

primary.notes: Teal primary in app chrome — coral is reserved for the rare conversion moment (upgrade, publish)

cta.notes: At most one coral button per screen

destructive.confirmNotes: Destructive actions always get a confirm step; the confirm button may fill #d63c2f

content.maxWidth: fluid — no container cap in app UI

table.border: 1px solid rgba(10, 61, 58, 0.06) (horizontal only — no vertical rules)
table.emptyState: centered, 48px icon in #A1E4DB, one-line explanation, one action button — an empty screen is an invitation to act

default.notes: xs shadow only — app UI doesn't float

metric.structure: label (11px uppercase) → value (metric, Space Grotesk) → delta (12px, status-colored with ▲▼)

badge.structure: 6px status dot + 12px label, 4px gap, 4px 10px padding, full-radius pill, status bg + border
badge.notes: Badges always pair dot + text — never color alone (color-blind safe)

input.label: 12px, weight 500, #1C5753, 6px below-gap
input.errorMessage: 12px #a32a1f, explains what to fix — never vague, never apologizes

toast.duration: 4s info/success, sticky for danger until dismissed

modal.notes: Focus trapped, Escape closes, focus returns to trigger on close

loading.spinner: see signature.arcProgress — the arc sweep, 20px inline / 32px page-level
loading.skeleton: pearl #e8f4f1 blocks, 1.5s shimmer, matching final layout exactly

spacing.notes: The only variant on a 4px grid — app density needs the finer step

accessibility.focusRing: 2px #25A497 ring offset 2px on every interactive element — app users live on the keyboard
accessibility.statusColor: Status is never conveyed by color alone — always dot + label
accessibility.contrast: All status text/bg pairs meet WCAG AA at 13px weight 500
accessibility.reducedMotion: Pulse, count-up, and shimmer all respect prefers-reduced-motion
